entry.title: Emacs学习笔记
entry.content: 缓冲区 作用 命令 函数名 保存当前缓冲区 C-x C-s save-buffer 保存所有缓冲区 C-x s save-some-buffers 列出所有缓冲区 C-x C-b list-buffers 切换缓冲区 C-x b 杀死缓冲区 C-x k kill-buffer 文件 作用 命令 函数名 打开文件 C-x C-f 只读方式打开 C-x C-r 读入另外一个文件代替当前buffer的文件 C-x C-v 文件编辑命令移动插入点 作用 命令 函数名 缓冲区开始 M-> beginning-of-buffer 缓冲区末尾 M-< end-of-buffer 上一屏 C-v scroll-up 下一屏 M-v scroll-down 段落开头 C-{ beginning-of-paragraph 段落结尾 C-} end-of-paragraph 到指定行 M-g g goto-line 行开头 C-a beginning-of-line 行结尾 C-e end-of-line 句子开头 M-a beginning-of-sentence 句子结尾 M-e end=of-sentence 上一行 C-p previous-line 下一行 C-n next-line 上一个单词 M-b backward-word 下一个单词 M-f forward-word 上一个字符 C-b back-char 下一个字符 C-f forward-char 引用插入、删除、撤销和重复 作用 命令 函数 引用插入 C-q <字符值> quote-insert 删除一个字符 C-d delete-char 删除一个单词 M-d kill-word 撤销 C-_ or C-/ undo 重复 C-u <次数> <命令> universal-argument 标记和复制 作用 命令 函数 删除一行 C-k kill-line 设定标记 C-Space or C-@ set-mark-command 全选 C-x h mark-whole-buffer 选取一页 C-x C-p mark-page 选取一段 M-h mark-paragraph 选取一个单词 M-@ mark-word 选取一页 C-x C-p mark-page 交换标记和光标位置 C-x C-x exchange-point-and-mark 将区域中字母改为大写 C-x C-u upcase-region 删除区块 C-w kill-region 复制区块 M-w kill-ring-save 粘贴 C-y yank 快速复制 作用 命令 函数 将区域中内容加入到一个buffer中 append-to-buffer 将区域中内容加入到一个buffer中，不过加入位置在该buffer的光标前 prepend-to-buffer 将区域中内容加入到一个buffer中，删除该buffer原有内容 copy-to-buffer 在该位置插入指定的buffer中所有内容 insert-buffer 将区域中内容复制到一个文件中 append-to-file 字符修改 作用 命令 函数 光标处单词首字母大写 M-c 光标处单词全部字母大写 M-u 光标处单词全部字母小写 M-l 补全 M-/ 交换光标处和之前的字符 C-t 交换光标处和之前的单词 M-t 搜索 作用 命令 函数 从光标处往后搜索 C-s 从光标处往前搜索 C-r (向下)光标位置的单词作为查找字符串 C-s C-w (向下)光标位置到行尾作为查找字符串 C-r C-y 非递增查找 C-s RET <查找字符串> RET 不受换行、空格、标点影响 C-s RET C-w 正则式向下查找 C-M-s 用向上查找命令就将上面命令的s替换为r 宏模式 作用 命令 函数 开始一个宏的定义 C-x ( 结束一个宏的定义 C-x ) 执行宏 C-x e 给最后一个宏命名 name-last-kbd-macro 在当前文件中插入一个已定义并命名过的宏 insert-kbd-macro 参考资料 Emacs 编辑环境，第 1 部分: 学习 Emacs 的基础知识 这里列出了EMACS 的一些常用命令 学习Emacs系列教程（五）：编辑（2）—— 标记 学习Emacs系列教程（六）：编辑（3）

entry.title: [Mac]在Emacs中安装AucTex
entry.content: 首先必要文件 安装Emacs：直接搜索Emacs即可，或者到这里下载即可。 安装MacTex：到这里下载 安装AucTex：打开Emacs，输入1M-x list-packages 确认，然后直接搜索AucTex这个包，按i标记，按x执行，就安装完成了 安装 cdlatex：复制cdlatex.el到”/Applications/Emacs.app/Contents/Resources/site-lisp”，然后打开.emacs加入以下代码 12345(add-hook 'LaTeX-mode-hook 'LaTeX-math-mode)(add-to-list 'load-path "/Applications/Emacs.app/Contents/Resources/site-lisp/")(add-hook 'LaTeX-mode-hook 'turn-on-cdlatex)(autoload 'cdlatex-mode "cdlatex" "CDLaTeX Mode" t)(autoload 'turn-on-cdlatex "cdlatex" "CDLaTeX Mode" nil) 开始使用基本快捷键 Complie: 1C-c C-c Preview Section: 1C-c C-p s Undo Preview: 1C-c C-p C-c s 问题解答Q1: TeX不能识别 Solution: 根据这里的描述，我们需要添加下列两个到PATH变量中 12/Library/TeX/texbin/usr/local/bin 但是图形界面的Emacs是不会全部加载bash的PATH的，所以直接在bash中修改PATH变量并没有用处，我们还需要添加如下代码到.emacs 12(setenv “PATH”(concat (getenv “PATH”) “:/Library/TeX/texbin:/usr/local/bin”)) Q2: AucTex 预览出错，错误提示为 1PDF2DSC sentinel: Wrong type argument: arrayp, nil Solution: 在.emacs中加入 1(setq preview-gs-command "/usr/local/bin/gs") 重新启动Emacs

entry.title: [Hexo]在GitHub上建Blog
entry.content: 需要的插件 安装Git 安装Node.js 安装[Hexo]：输入下列命令 1$ npm install -g hexo-cli 建立博客安装文件创建一个你喜欢的文件夹，比如Blog，这就是你博客的大本营了，在该文件夹根目录下运行 1$ hexo init 等待命令完成，你会发现文件夹里多了好多东西，我们先来试运行一下，以此输入命令 12$ hexo generate$ hexo server 然后打开浏览器，访问http://localhost:4000，你就会见到你的博客了 修改博客的基本属性打开根目录下的_config.yml，修改各种参数信息 所有参数的冒号后面都要加上一个空格 发表文章1$ hexo new "你的文章标题" 系统会提示创建了”…/source/_post/你的文章标题.md”。这个MD文件就是我们的文章的主体了，打开这个目录，你会发现里面已经有了”hello-world.md”这个文件。这就是刚刚在浏览器里看到的那个唯一的一篇博文。要删除一篇文章，就直接将对应的MD文件删除即可 发布上网Github首先你要注册一个GitHub账号，我们发布的原理是GitHub提供一个叫GitHub Page的网页，它对应的网址是”username.github.io”。而它对应的仓库是”usename.github.io”。所以你的用户名非常重要，注册的时候请谨慎选取 部署添加部署信息打开_config.yml，找到 添加SSH KEY安装部署器运行如下命令 1$ npm install hexo-deployer-git --save 参考链接 教你免费搭建个人博客，Hexo&Github Hexo官方文档 - 部署 手把手教你使用Hexo + Github Pages搭建个人独立博客 GitHub Help - SSH Next 主题

entry.title: Remarks on Completion of Rings
entry.content: Definition of CompletionGiven a ring $R$, not necessarily Noetherian, we could always define its completion $\hat{R}^I$ with respect to an ideal $I\subset R$. Cauchy SequencesOne way to define this is using Cauchy sequence: A sequence $(r_1,r_2,\cdots,r_n,\cdots)$ is a Cauchy sequence if$$\forall n \ \ \exists N \ \text{ such that } \forall i,j\geq N, r_i-r_j\in I^n$$while a null sequence $(r_1,r_2,\cdots,r_n,\cdots)$ is a sequence converges to $0$, that is,$$\forall n \ \ \exists N \ \text{ such that } \forall i\geq N, r_i\in I^n$$ The completion $\hat{R}^I$ is defined to be the ring of all Cauchy sequences modulo the ideal generated by null sequences. Inverse LimitThe completion $\hat{R}^I$ could also be defined as an inverse limit of the system$$R/I\leftarrow R/I^2\leftarrow \cdots \leftarrow R/I^n\leftarrow$$ Completion & LocalizationMaximal ideals of $\hat{R}^I$We have following theorem TheoremThe kernel of the natural map $\hat{R}^I\rightarrow R/I$ is contained in the Jacobson ideal of $\hat{R}^I$. ProofWe use the inverse limit construction. So each element $r$ in $\hat{R}^I$ is denoted by a sequence where $(r_1,r_2,…,r_i,…)$ such that $r_i\in R/I^i$ maps to $r_j\in R/I^j$ for any $i>j$. If $r\in \hat{R}^I$ is in the kernel, then $r={0,r_2,…,r_i,…}$ where each $r_k$ is an element in $I/I^k$. Let $v=(v_1,v_2,…,v_i,…)$ be a sequence such that $v_{i}=1-r_i+r_i^2-\cdots+(-1)^i r_i^i$. Then the image of $v_n$ in $R/I^{n-1}$ is $v_{n-1}$. So $v\in\hat{R}^I$ and we have $(1+r)v=1$. So the kernel is contained in the Jacobson ideal. $\blacksquare$ A direct corollary from this theorem is that we have 1-1 correspondence between maximal ideals of $\hat{R}^I$ and maximal ideals of $R/I$. In particular, if $R/I$ is quasilocal (local but not necessarily Noetherian), then $\hat{R}^I$ is quasilocal. Completion Commutes with LocalizationIf $I=Q$ is a maximal ideal of $R$, then $\hat{R}^Q$ is a quasilocal ring. If we localize $R$ at $Q$ and then take its completion, we also get a quasilocal ring $\hat{R_Q}^Q$. In fact, these two rings are isomorphic to each other as we have following lemma LemmaIf $Q$ is a maximal ideal of $R$, then $R_Q/Q^nR_Q\simeq R/Q^n$ ProofThe only prime ideals in $R/Q^n$ is $Q$ as $Q^n$ is $Q$-primary. So $R/Q^n$ is a quasilocal ring. Therefore the image $R-Q$ is invertible in $R/Q^n$. So we have the desired inverse for the natural map $R/Q^n\rightarrow R_Q/Q^nR_Q$. $\blacksquare$

entry.title: Vanishing of Sections
entry.content:
Original ProblemThe problem comes from an exercise (Chapter 1 Ex 3.3(c)) in Hartshorne Show that if $\varphi(X)$ is dense in $Y$, then the map $\varphi^*_P$ is injective for all $P$. Here $X$ and $Y$ are assumed to be varieties. But this could be generalized to morphisms of locally ringed spaces. If $(\varphi,\varphi^\sharp):(X,\mathscr O_X)ightarrow (Y,\mathscr O_Y)$ is a morphism of locally ringed space, $\varphi(X)$ is dense in $Y$ and $X$ is irreducible, then $\varphi^\sharp_P$ is injective for any $P\in X$ Vanishing of SectionsBefore prove this, let’s discuss a little bit about the definition of ‘’section vanishes’’. Two DefinitionsRecall the usual definition of support of a section in a given sheaf $\mathcal F$: DefinitionThe support of $s\in\mathcal F(X)$ is $	ext{supp}(s)={P\in X|s_P
ot=0}$ where $s_P$ is the image of $s$ in the stalk $\mathcal F_P$ The support of a section is always closed because if $P
ot\in 	ext{supp}(s)$, then there is some open subset $U$ containing $P$ such that $s|_U=0$. Therefore $U\cap	ext{supp}(s)=\emptyset$, which shows that $	ext{supp}(s)$ is closed. However, recall the definition of the support of a function, it’s the preimage of $k^*$, which, in particular, is open. If we are considering the sheaf of functions here, then what’s the ‘’correct’’ definition of support of a section (which is a function) corresponding to the definition of support of a function? If you think about this, the answer seems to be immediate. A function $f$ vanishes at a point $P$ when its function value is zero at that point. Think about the definition of the stalk at that point, the image of $f$ has to lie in the maximal ideal of the local ring $\mathcal F_P$. This enlightens us to give following defintion DefinitionThe support of $s\in\mathcal F(X)$ as a function is $	ext{fupp}(s)={P\in X|s_P
ot\in m_{X,P}}$ where $m_{X,P}$ is the maximal ideal in the stalk $\mathcal F_P$ Topological PropertiesWe already saw that $	ext{supp}(s)$ is closed. It turns out that $	ext{fupp}(s)$ is open. Because if $P\in	ext{fupp}(s)$, then $s_P$ is not in the maximal ideal $m_{X,P}$, so it has an inverse $(t,U)$. That is, $st|_U=1$. So they are inverse to each other in any stalk at the points in $U$. Therefore $p\in U\in 	ext{fupp}(c)$. It’s also clear from the definition that $	ext{fupp}(s)\subset	ext{supp}(s)$. For notational reason, let’s denote the complement of $	ext{supp}(s)$ (resp. $	ext{fupp}(s)$) by $	ext{szero}(s)$ (resp. $	ext{fzero}(s)$). Then we already established $	ext{fupp}(s)$ is open and contained in the closed subset $	ext{supp}(s)$ $	ext{szero}(s)$ is open and contained in the closed subset $	ext{fzero}(s)$ The behaviour of the functional support and zero locus is very similar to the behaviour of functions we know. We have following theorems TheoremIf $(\varphi,\varphi^\sharp):(X,\mathscr O_X)ightarrow (Y,\mathscr O_Y)$ is a morphism of locally ringed spaces, then$$ \varphi^{-1}(	ext{fupp}(s))=	ext{fupp}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$$ It’s immediate from the theorem that $$\varphi^{-1}(	ext{fzero}(s))=	ext{fzero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$$ Meanwhile, the behaviour of section is not that good TheoremIf $(\varphi,\varphi^\sharp):(X,\mathscr O_X)ightarrow (Y,\mathscr O_Y)$ is a morphism of locally ringed spaces, then$$ \varphi^{-1}(	ext{szero}(s))\subset	ext{szero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))\subset \varphi^{-1}(	ext{fupp}(s))=	ext{fupp}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$$ The proof of these two theorems comes from the commutative diagram$equire{AMScd}$ egin{CD} \mathscr O_Y(U) @>>> \mathscr O_X(\varphi^{-1}(U)) @. s @>>> \varphi^\sharp (s) \ @VVV @VVV @VVV @VVV \ \mathscr O_{Y,\varphi(P)} @>>> \mathscr O_{X,P} @. s_{\varphi(P)} @>>> \varphi^\sharp(s)_P\end{CD} If $s_{\varphi(P)}$ is zero (resp. contained in the maximal ideal), then $\varphi^\sharp(s)$ maps to zero (resp. contained in the maximal ideal). So $P\in	ext{szero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$ (resp. $P\in	ext{fzero}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$). For th converse direction, if $s_{\varphi(P)}$ is not contained in the maximal ideal), then $\varphi^\sharp(s)$ maps to a unit. So $P\in	ext{fupp}(\varphi^\sharp(s))$ Proof of Original ProblemNow we have enough tools to prove our original problem ProofFor any point $P\in X$, let $(f,U)$ be a nonzero element in the stalk $\mathscr O_{Y,\varphi(P)}$. Replace $Y$ with $U$, then $f
eq 0$ means that $\varphi(P)\in	ext{supp}(f)$. We want to prove that $(\varphi^\sharp(f),\varphi^{-1}(U))$ is not zero in the stalk $\mathscr O_{X,P}$. That is equivalent to $P\in	ext{supp}(\varphi^\sharp(f))$ Denote $\varphi^\sharp(f)$ by $g$. Assume that $g
ot\in	ext{supp}(g)$, then since $	ext{supp}(g)$ is closed, there is some open subset $V$ such that $p\in V\subset 	ext{szero}(g)$. On the other hand, $\varphi(X)$ is dense in $Y$, so it’s dense in $U$. Therefore $\varphi(X)\cap 	ext{fupp}(f)
eq \emptyset$. So there is some point $Q$ such that $\varphi(Q)\in	ext{fupp}(g)$. But $	ext{fupp}(g)$ is open, so there is some open subset $V’$ such that $Q\in V’\subset 	ext{fupp}(g)$. Note that $X$ is irreducible, $V\cap V’
eq\emptyset$, but this is a contradiction as $	ext{fupp}(g)\cap	ext{fzero}(g)=\emptyset$ and $	ext{szero}(g)\subset 	ext{fzero}(g)$. $ lacksquare$
